digest.top_line: ENZARAD: adding enza to definitive RT + ADT missed 1° MFS (8y 74 vs 72%, HR 0.88, p=0.34); cN1/pelvic-RT subgroups signal benefit (HR 0.43/0.47), hypothesis-generating.
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day's only data: ENZARAD found no MFS gain from adding 24mo enzalutamide to definitive EBRT + long-term ADT in high-risk localized disease (HR 0.88, ns). Node-positive and pelvic-RT subgroups favored enza (HR 0.43/0.47), but nominal and unadjusted for multiplicity — hypothesis-generating for intensifying node+ definitive RT.

sites.intro: One trial: ENZARAD tests ARSI intensification on top of definitive EBRT + long-term ADT in high-risk localized disease, with the actionable signal confined to node-positive/pelvic-RT pts.

studies.name: ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303)
studies.tldr: 1° MFS not met (8y 74% vs 72%, HR 0.88, p=0.34), but pelvic-RT/cN1 subgroups showed enzalutamide benefit (HR 0.47/0.43).
details: 📊 PFS favored enza: 8y 67% vs 62%, HR 0.78 (0.61-0.99), p=0.044 (nominal)

details.text: 📊 Enzalutamide effect by prespecified subgroup (MFS + OS HRs)

details: 🔍 Phase 3 RCT, N=802, 8 countries, recruited 2014-2018; median f/u 8y. Enza 160mg ×24mo vs NSAA ×6mo, both + 24mo LHRH + EBRT
🔍 RT: prostate 78Gy or 46Gy + brachy boost; cN1 required pelvic nodes (46Gy elective + boost to gross nodes), optional/pre-declared for cN0

details.text: 🔗 vs STAMPEDE abiraterone: weaker overall HR reflects a lower-risk ENZARAD cohort

details: ⚠️ Subgroup benefits nominal, unadjusted for multiplicity; 5 prespecified subgroups tested by interaction p, hypothesis-generating
⚠️ Pelvic-RT group higher-risk than no-pelvic-RT (28% vs 0% N1, 62% vs 49% G9-10); benefit may track risk selection, not RT field

verdict.rationale: Well-powered phase 3, prespecified 1° MFS negative: confirms no routine enzalutamide intensification for most. cN1/pelvic-RT subgroup benefits are nominal, unadjusted, not practice-defining.
verdict.audience: High-risk clinically localized/locally-advanced prostate, EBRT + 2y ADT…

studies.significance: The pelvic-RT subgroup, not the negative primary, is the RT read: enzalutamide cut MFS events only where pelvic nodal RT was planned (HR 0.47, 0.29-0.76) or cN1 (HR 0.43, 0.20-0.92). But that group was higher-risk (28% vs 0% N1, 62% vs 49% G9-10), so selection confounds the field signal. Moves intensification toward whole-pelvis/cN1 pts.
studies.monday_clinic: In cN1 or whole-pelvis-RT-planned high-risk localized prostate pts, this supports adding enzalutamide over an active-NSAA control; it does not extend to cN0 prostate-only pts, who showed no MFS benefit.

primary_endpoint.name: Metastasis-free survival
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: 8y 74% vs 72%; 95% CI 0.67-1.15, p=0.34; 1° endpoint not met

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Phase 3** open-label RCT, N=802, 8 countries, recruited 2014-2018; median follow-up **8 years**. OS was the original primary endpoint, switched to **MFS** mid-study because deaths were fewer than anticipated.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: High-risk clinically localized or locally-advanced prostate suitable for EBRT. **90% Gleason 8-10**, 36% PSA >20 ng/ml, **12% cN1** by conventional imaging. 40% planned for pelvic RT, 8% for a brachy boost.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Experimental: **enzalutamide 160 mg daily ×24 mo** + LHRH agonist ×24 mo. Control: conventional NSAA ×6 mo + LHRH ×24 mo. Hormones started 16 wks before RT.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Prostate **78 Gy** or 46 Gy plus brachy boost. Pelvic nodes **46 Gy elective + boost to gross nodes**, required for cN1 and optional/pre-declared for cN0. Intensive real-time QA on plans.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: Primary: **metastasis-free survival** (80% power for HR 0.67). Secondary: OS, CSS, PSA PFS, clinical PFS, castration resistance, HRQoL, AEs, cost-effectiveness.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **1° MFS not met**: HR 0.88 (p=0.34). PFS favored enzalutamide (HR 0.78, p=0.044); OS ns (HR 0.87). The overall null reflects a favorable-risk cohort diluting a real cN1/pelvic-RT signal.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Overall MFS HR weaker than **STAMPEDE** abiraterone (**0.88 vs 0.53**), consistent with ENZARAD's lower-risk cohort (11% vs 39% cN1, median PSA 14 vs 35). The cN1 MFS benefit mirrors STAMPEDE's node-positive signal.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents high-risk clinically localized or locally-advanced prostate treated with modern EBRT + 2y ADT. Does not represent cN0 prostate-only pts, who derived no MFS benefit.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Subgroup benefits are nominal, unadjusted for multiplicity, and confounded by baseline-risk differences between the pelvic-RT and no-pelvic-RT groups. Open-label; original OS endpoint underpowered by a low event rate.

open_questions: Which pelvic-RT subgroups drive the enzalutamide benefit
Biomarkers to select localized prostate pts needing AR intensification
Does pelvic nodal RT enable the enzalutamide distant-mets benefit

related_trials.brief_title: Randomized Trial of Prostate Only or Pelvic RT in High Risk Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
Evidence to justify the use of the pelvic field is lacking for men with high risk localized prostate cancer. An additional randomized study is needed in men with a potential risk of nodal involvement to test the hypothesis that the use of the pelvic field contributed to the benefit observed in those studies.

This trial aims to compare the outcomes with whole pelvis RT and Prostate alone RT in patients with High risk, non metastatic Prostate cancer with a Lymph nodal \>20% with Primary 5 year…

related_trials.brief_title: A Trial of 5 Fraction Prostate SBRT Versus 5 Fraction Prostate and Pelvic Nodal SBRT
related_trials.brief_summary: This study will compare the safety and efficacy of curative radiotherapy to the prostate and lymph glands given in 5 visits to that of prostate alone radiotherapy given in 5 visits, in men with high risk localised prostate cancer.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

papers.title: ESMO 2025: Randomized Phase III Trial of Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) with Radiation Therapy with or without Enzalutamide for High Risk, Clinically Localized Prostate Cancer: ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303)
papers.abstract: ESMO 2025 ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303), randomized phase II trial of ADT + radiation therapy +/- enzalutamide, localized prostate cancer.
